Decision time point: 2005-01-15 - only information available on or before this date is admissible as evidence.

        ## Observable Catalog (closed vocabulary - proxies MUST come from this list)

        [AVAILABLE_COLUMNS] = [GDPC1, CPIAUCSL, PAYEMS, INDPRO, RSAFS, UNRATE, HOUST, FEDFUNDS]

        Binding rule: candidate_proxies may only copy exact tokens from [AVAILABLE_COLUMNS].
        If the desired economic proxy is not present, do not invent a replacement name.

        Series ID   | Domain    | Freq      | Unit                          | Rev.Risk
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GDPC1       | demand    | quarterly | Billions of Chained 2017 Dollars | high
CPIAUCSL    | monetary  | monthly   | Index 1982-1984=100            | low
PAYEMS      | labor     | monthly   | Thousands of Persons           | high
INDPRO      | supply    | monthly   | Index 2017=100                 | medium
RSAFS       | demand    | monthly   | Millions of Dollars            | medium
UNRATE      | labor     | monthly   | Percent                        | low
HOUST       | demand    | monthly   | Thousands of Units             | medium
FEDFUNDS    | monetary  | monthly   | Percent per Annum              | low

        ## Target Panel Summary (S&P 500 forward 20-day returns)

          Ticker        : ^GSPC
  Data mode     : real_data
  Source        : Yahoo Finance via yfinance
  Target        : direction_20d = sign(log(Close[t+20 trading days] / Close[t]))
  Date range    : 2005-01-03 -> 2024-12-31
  Total sessions: 5,033
  Positive days : 65.5%  (20d forward return >= 0)
  Negative days : 34.1%
  Pos/Neg ratio : 1.925
  Latest macro snapshot used for prompt context:
    decision_date_used: 2005-01-15
    model_ready_panel_rows: 1348709
    - CPIAUCSL: vintage_date=2004-11-01, vintage_value=191.2, revised_value=191.7
    - FEDFUNDS: vintage_date=2004-12-01, vintage_value=2.16, revised_value=2.16
    - GDPC1: vintage_date=2004-07-01, vintage_value=10891.0, revised_value=15512.619
    - HOUST: vintage_date=2004-11-01, vintage_value=1771.0, revised_value=1782.0
    - INDPRO: vintage_date=2004-12-01, vintage_value=117.813, revised_value=95.5365
    - PAYEMS: vintage_date=2004-12-01, vintage_value=132266.0, revised_value=132630.0
    - RSAFS: vintage_date=2004-12-01, vintage_value=349436.0, revised_value=324672.0
    - UNRATE: vintage_date=2004-12-01, vintage_value=5.4, revised_value=5.4

        ## Sketch Schema (JSON Schema - all sketches must conform)

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ## Task

        Generate exactly 8 hypothesis sketches as a JSON array.
        Assign hypothesis_id values as H_001, H_002, ..., H_008.

        Requirements:
        - Cover at least 3 different dominant_domains across the 8 sketches.
        - Include at least 1 sketch per mechanism type:
            * Always-on trend signal (candidate_trigger = "none", candidate_interactions = false)
            * Threshold-triggered signal (candidate_trigger describes a crossing condition)
            * Interaction-driven signal (candidate_interactions = true, >= 2 proxies)
            * Regime-conditional signal (regime_hint is specific, not "any regime")
        - Vary candidate_horizon_hint across 5, 20, and 60.
        - Use correct JSON types: booleans must be true/false (not "true"/"false").

        Output ONLY the JSON array - nothing else.

        ## Few-shot Example (do NOT copy H_000 - it is a format reference only)

        The sketch below illustrates a correctly formatted output. Your sketches must use
        H_001, H_002, ... (not H_000). Every field shown must be present and correctly typed.

        [
          {
            "hypothesis_id": "H_000",
            "dominant_domain": "monetary",
            "mechanism_narrative": "A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.",
            "direction": "+",
            "candidate_proxies": ["FEDFUNDS"],
            "candidate_horizon_hint": 20,
            "candidate_trigger": "none",
            "candidate_interactions": false,
            "evidence_refs": ["series:FEDFUNDS"],
            "regime_hint": "monetary easing cycle",
            "failure_condition": "Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05)."
          }
        ]

You are a macro-financial researcher. Given a point-in-time evidence snapshot
(macro series values, market data), you propose structured mechanism hypotheses
explaining how macroeconomic signals drive S&P 500 forward returns.

Each hypothesis must be a concrete, falsifiable causal claim - not a vague narrative.
It must specify which observable series drive the mechanism, in what direction,
over what horizon, and under what conditions it would fail.

OUTPUT FORMAT: A single JSON array of hypothesis objects. Nothing else - no prose,
no markdown fences, no commentary. Every field in the provided schema is required.
candidate_proxies must use exact series IDs from the observable catalog.
candidate_interactions must be a JSON boolean (true/false), not a string.
direction must be "+" or "-" only.

Decision time point: 2005-02-15 - only information available on or before this date is admissible as evidence.

        [AVAILABLE_COLUMNS] = [GDPC1, CPIAUCSL, PAYEMS, INDPRO, RSAFS, UNRATE, HOUST, FEDFUNDS]

        If the desired economic proxy is not present, do not invent a replacement name.

UNRATE      | labor     | monthly   | Percent                        | low

FEDFUNDS    | monetary  | monthly   | Percent per Annum              | low

explaining how macroeconomic signals drive S&P 500 forward returns.

Each hypothesis must be a concrete, falsifiable causal claim - not a vague narrative.
It must specify which observable series drive the mechanism, in what direction,
over what horizon, and under what conditions it would fail.

OUTPUT FORMAT: A single JSON array of hypothesis objects. Nothing else - no prose,
no markdown fences, no commentary. Every field in the provided schema is required.
candidate_proxies must use exact series IDs from the observable catalog.
candidate_interactions must be a JSON boolean (true/false), not a string.
direction must be "+" or "-" only.

Decision time point: 2005-04-15 - only information available on or before this date is admissible as evidence.

        ## Observable Catalog (closed vocabulary - proxies MUST come from this list)

        [AVAILABLE_COLUMNS] = [GDPC1, CPIAUCSL, PAYEMS, INDPRO, RSAFS, UNRATE, HOUST, FEDFUNDS]

        Binding rule: candidate_proxies may only copy exact tokens from [AVAILABLE_COLUMNS].
        If the desired economic proxy is not present, do not invent a replacement name.

        Series ID   | Domain    | Freq      | Unit                          | Rev.Risk
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GDPC1       | demand    | quarterly | Billions of Chained 2017 Dollars | high
CPIAUCSL    | monetary  | monthly   | Index 1982-1984=100            | low
PAYEMS      | labor     | monthly   | Thousands of Persons           | high
INDPRO      | supply    | monthly   | Index 2017=100                 | medium
RSAFS       | demand    | monthly   | Millions of Dollars            | medium
UNRATE      | labor     | monthly   | Percent                        | low
HOUST       | demand    | monthly   | Thousands of Units             | medium
FEDFUNDS    | monetary  | monthly   | Percent per Annum              | low

        ## Sketch Schema (JSON Schema - all sketches must conform)

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ## Task

        Generate exactly 8 hypothesis sketches as a JSON array.
        Assign hypothesis_id values as H_001, H_002, ..., H_008.

        Requirements:
        - Cover at least 3 different dominant_domains across the 8 sketches.
        - Include at least 1 sketch per mechanism type:
            * Always-on trend signal (candidate_trigger = "none", candidate_interactions = false)
            * Threshold-triggered signal (candidate_trigger describes a crossing condition)
            * Interaction-driven signal (candidate_interactions = true, >= 2 proxies)
            * Regime-conditional signal (regime_hint is specific, not "any regime")
        - Vary candidate_horizon_hint across 5, 20, and 60.
        - Use correct JSON types: booleans must be true/false (not "true"/"false").

        Output ONLY the JSON array - nothing else.

        ```json
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",

    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },

      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        {

        ```json
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful.       "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
        ```

        ```json
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [

        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],

    },

      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
        ```

        ```json
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,

      "type": "string",
      "enum": [

    },

This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."

      "minItems": 1
    },

      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes.     "failure_condition": {
        ```

        {
        ```json
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ## Task

        Generate exactly 8 hypothesis sketches as a JSON array.
        Assign hypothesis_id values as H_001, H_002, ..., H_008.

        Requirements:
        - Cover at least 3 different dominant_domains across the 8 sketches.
        - Include at least 1 sketch per mechanism type:
            * Always-on trend signal (candidate_trigger = "none", candidate_interactions = false)
            * Threshold-triggered signal (candidate_trigger describes a crossing condition)
            * Interaction-driven signal (candidate_interactions = true, >= 2 proxies)
            * Regime-conditional signal (regime_hint is specific, not "any regime")
        - Vary candidate_horizon_hint across 5, 20, and 60.
        - Use correct JSON types: booleans must be true/false (not "true"/"false").

        Output ONLY the JSON array - nothing else.

You are a macro-financial researcher. Given a point-in-time evidence snapshot
(macro series values, market data), you propose structured mechanism hypotheses
explaining how macroeconomic signals drive S&P 500 forward returns.

Each hypothesis must be a concrete, falsifiable causal claim - not a vague narrative.
It must specify which observable series drive the mechanism, in what direction,
over what horizon, and under what conditions it would fail.

OUTPUT FORMAT: A single JSON array of hypothesis objects. Nothing else - no prose,
no markdown fences, no commentary. Every field in the provided schema is required.
candidate_proxies must use exact series IDs from the observable catalog.
candidate_interactions must be a JSON boolean (true/false), not a string.
direction must be "+" or "-" only.

Decision time point: 2010-12-15 - only information available on or before this date is admissible as evidence.

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",

      ],

    },

      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,

    },

      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60

      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },

      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
        ```
