        ## Sketch Schema (JSON Schema - all sketches must conform)

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}

  "required_fields": [

    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

    "candidate_horizon_hint",

    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

    "candidate_interactions": {

    },

      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}

      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },

      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}

      "type": "string",

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        - Vary candidate_horizon_hint across 5, 20, and 60.

        Output ONLY the JSON array - nothing else.

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}

        {
  "required_fields": [

    "dominant_domain",

    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,

      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected.       "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}

      "type": "string",

    },

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

        ## Task

        Generate exactly 8 hypothesis sketches as a JSON array.
        Assign hypothesis_id values as H_001, H_002, ..., H_008.

        Requirements:
        - Cover at least 3 different dominant_domains across the 8 sketches.

            * Interaction-driven signal (candidate_interactions = true, >= 2 proxies)
            * Regime-conditional signal (regime_hint is specific, not "any regime")
        - Vary candidate_horizon_hint across 5, 20, and 60.
        - Use correct JSON types: booleans must be true/false (not "true"/"false").

        Output ONLY the JSON array - nothing else.

explaining how macroeconomic signals drive S&P 500 forward returns.

It must specify which observable series drive the mechanism, in what direction,
over what horizon, and under what conditions it would fail.

direction must be "+" or "-" only.

        ```json
        {
  "required_fields": [
    "hypothesis_id",
    "dominant_domain",
    "mechanism_narrative",
    "direction",
    "candidate_proxies",
    "candidate_horizon_hint",
    "candidate_trigger",
    "candidate_interactions",
    "evidence_refs",
    "regime_hint",
    "failure_condition"
  ],
  "field_constraints": {
    "hypothesis_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^H_[0-9]{3,}$",
      "description": "Unique sketch identifier. Format: H_NNN (zero-padded integer). Assigned by the caller; LLM must not invent colliding IDs."
    },
    "dominant_domain": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "demand",
        "supply",
        "monetary",
        "labor"
      ],
      "description": "Primary macro domain this hypothesis belongs to, drawn from the observable_catalog dominant_macro_domain vocabulary. Determines the mechanism-domain cell in the revision-sensitivity heatmap."
    },
    "mechanism_narrative": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 40,
      "maxLength": 400,
      "description": "Plain-language description of the causal or statistical mechanism. Must be falsifiable: it should be possible to state what observable pattern would confirm or refute it. Do not use hedged language ('may', 'might', 'could potentially')."
    },
    "direction": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "+",
        "-"
      ],
      "description": "Predicted direction of the forward return over the target horizon. '+' means the mechanism predicts positive SPX returns; '-' means negative. Must be unambiguous at compile time."
    },
    "candidate_proxies": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Ordered list of ALFRED series IDs to use as proxy variables. Must be drawn exclusively from the observable_catalog closed vocabulary. Maximum K_proxy=4 entries (budget constraint from operator_closure.yaml).",
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "candidate_horizon_hint": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [
        5,
        20,
        60
      ],
      "description": "Suggested prediction horizon in trading days. Corresponds to approximately 1 week (5), 1 month (20), or 1 quarter (60). The instantiation grid in Phase 2 Workflow E will expand this into specific horizon_grid entries."
    },
    "candidate_trigger": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Activation condition for the signal. Use the literal string 'none' if the mechanism is always-on. Otherwise describe the threshold or regime condition that must be met before the signal is considered active (e.g., 'UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months', 'FEDFUNDS > 4.5%'). This field maps to trigger_grid in the compiled hypothesis object."
    },
    "candidate_interactions": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "True if the mechanism requires the joint behaviour of two or more proxy series (i.e., the signal is not decomposable into independent single-series effects). Setting true triggers interaction_driven family assignment in Workflow B. Max interaction order controlled by K_int=2 in operator_closure.yaml."
    },
    "evidence_refs": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "References to supporting evidence. Each entry should identify a data series, document, or empirical observation visible at the decision time point. Format: 'series:<ID>', 'doc:<slug>', or 'fact:<description>'. Minimum 1 entry required.",
      "minItems": 1
    },
    "regime_hint": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 4,
      "description": "Description of the macro regime in which this mechanism is expected to be active or most powerful. Used in Phase 2 Workflow B to assign regime_conditional family when the mechanism's direction flips across regimes. Examples: 'monetary tightening cycle', 'late-cycle expansion', 'post-shock recovery', 'any regime'."
    },
    "failure_condition": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 20,
      "description": "Explicit falsification condition: what observable pattern or data outcome would cause this hypothesis to be rejected. Must be specific enough to be testable in Phase 3 walk-forward falsification. Examples: 'SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal', 'Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window'."
    }
  }
}
        ```

    "candidate_horizon_hint",
